postes.allstar_msg: The reigning champion of the Tour de France has a strong team by his side and he’s ready to storm the uphill finish.

postes.allstar_msg: The 4-time winner of the Tour de France was delighted with his climbing legs on Saturday. Stage 2’s finale seems tailor-made for his punch.
A supreme puncheur, the Flying Dutchman has been targeting this day to repeat his victories on day 2 of the 2021 and 2025 Tours.
As he gifted stage 2 to Isaac del Toro, the Slovenian star showed his strength ahead of the first altitude finish.
Already a stage winner, the Mexican wonderkid has many assets to succeed: punch, tactical skills, and a strong relationship with Pogacar.
One of the most powerful riders in the peloton, the Flying Dutchman can win the stage in a small sprint group or even solo.
He is currently the best sprinter, with five victories this season and ten wins in the last four editions of the Tour de France.
Having won three of the last four stages featuring the Tourmalet, the Slovenian demonstrated at Les Angles exactly what he is looking to confirm in this stage: his supremacy in the mountains.

The Mexican prodigy has already raised his arms in victory at Montjuïc and subsequently helped Pogačar do the same at Les Angles. A strong performance in the Pyrenees will be crucial for the white jersey.
The Dane reminded everyone (both at Montjuïc and at Les Angles) that he remains Pogacar’s main rival. He believes in his chances of dethroning the reigning champion… and so does his team.
The world’s current top sprinter is out for revenge after a disappointing result in Pau (5th). Bordeaux, where he is the most recent winner (2023), is the ideal venue.
